title: 10 best herbal teas for hematuria
intro: Hematuria, or blood in urine, can result from various medical conditions. While addressing the underlying cause is crucial, certain herbal teas can complement the treatment. These teas include chamomile, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the urinary tract. Corn silk tea is another beneficial option, as it is known to promote urinary tract health and may decrease blood in urine. Lastly, buchu tea, with its diuretic effects, can help flush out the urinary system and potentially reduce hematuria symptoms. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatments.

teas.tea_desc: Cornsilk tea contains diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it an effective remedy for hematuria. The diuretic nature of cornsilk increases urine production, which helps to flush out the urinary tract, reducing the risk of infection. The anti-inflammatory property helps alleviate inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby minimizing irritation. Moreover, cornsilk's antiseptic qualities inhibit bacterial growth, preventing the onset of urinary tract infections that can cause hematuria. As a result, cornsilk tea offers a holistic approach, addressing the various causes of hematuria while promoting a healthy urinary system.
tea_parts: Seeds: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, corn silk seeds contribute to reducing inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating hematuria.
Stems: Containing antiseptic and diuretic properties, corn silk stems encourage urination, flushing out toxins, and promoting healing in hematuria conditions.
Leaves: Packed with flavonoids and tannins, corn silk leaves possess astringent qualities that help constrict blood vessels, reducing blood in urine.
Flowers: Abundant in potassium and magnesium, corn silk flowers aid in soothing the urinary system, easing hematuria symptoms by reducing bleeding.
tea_constituents: Manganese: Essential for blood clotting and reducing inflammation, helping to control hematuria.
Potassium: Helps regulate kidney function and fluid balance, reducing the occurrence of hematuria.
Vitamin C: Promotes iron absorption and acts as an antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress in the urinary tract.
Magnesium: Improves kidney function, reduces spasms, and alleviates symptoms of hematuria.
Tannins: Known for their astringent properties, tannins can help reduce bleeding and heal damaged tissues in the urinary tract.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help manage and alleviate hematuria symptoms.
tea_recipe: Obtain 2 cups of fresh corn silk and rinse it thoroughly to remove impurities.
Boil 4 cups of water in a pot, then add the clean corn silk and reduce heat to a simmer.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove corn silk particles and retain the liquid.
Drink the warm tea up to three times a day for best results, ideally between meals.

teas.tea_desc: Horsetail tea aids hematuria, or blood in urine, due to its high equisetin content. Equisetin boasts potent anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. These attributes promote urinary tract health by reducing inflammation and increasing urine flow, thereby eliminating blood. Additionally, horsetail contains silicic acid, which strengthens blood vessel walls and minimizes the likelihood of ruptures that cause hematuria. Finally, horsetail's antioxidants protect tissues from oxidative stress and support the kidneys' filtering process, discouraging hematuria.
tea_parts: Stems: The stems of horsetail tea plant are rich in silica and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation in the urinary tract and stop hematuria.
Leaves: The leaves of horsetail tea plant contain tannins and flavonoids that have astringent properties, which can help stop bleeding and heal the urinary tract, alleviating hematuria.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of horsetail tea plant are high in antioxidants and have diuretic properties, which can help flush out toxins and reduce inflammation, relieving hematuria.
tea_constituents: Silica: Strengthens connective tissues and capillaries, reducing the likelihood of blood in urine.
Potassium: Promotes healthy kidney function, aiding in the prevention of hematuria.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, protecting the urinary tract and reducing inflammation.
Saponins: Enhance the absorption of nutrients, improving overall urinary health.
Tannins: Provide astringent properties, helping to reduce bleeding and soothe tissues in the urinary tract.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water to 212°F (100°C) for 1-2 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons (2-4 grams) of dried horsetail herb to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the horsetail herb.
Consume the tea while it's still warm, up to 3 times a day for no more than 1 week.

teas.tea_desc: Ginger tea aids hematuria by possessing strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby controlling bleeding. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, inhibit clotting and improve blood flow, which helps stop bleeding in the urine. Moreover, ginger's diuretic effect increases urine production, flushing out the urinary system and helping to eliminate blood clots. Furthermore, ginger tea has antibacterial properties that fight infections causing hematuria. Thus, drinking ginger tea provides a natural, soothing remedy for hematuria symptoms.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: Rich in bioactive compounds, rhizomes help reduce inflammation and improve kidney function, addressing hematuria.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, leaves assist in cleansing the urinary system, reducing the risk of hematuria.
Flowers: Packed with vitamins and minerals, flowers support healthy blood flow, reducing the occurrence of hematuria.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and nutrients, seeds promote overall urinary tract health, decreasing hematuria symptoms.
tea_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate hematuria.
Shogaols: Known for their antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, shogaols can help protect the urinary tract from damage, preventing hematuria caused by infections or irritants.
Paradols: With similar properties to gingerols, paradols help inhibit inflammation, providing relief from hematuria due to inflammatory conditions or injuries.
Zingerone: As a natural analgesic, zingerone can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hematuria, while its antioxidant effects support overall urinary tract health.
tea_recipe: Peel and grate 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root.
Boil 2 cups of water, then add grated ginger, reduce heat, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the ginger tea into a cup, discarding the solid pieces.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice for flavor (if desired).
Serve the ginger tea hot and drink up to three times a day for hematuria relief.

teas.tea_desc: Buchu tea contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help reduce inflammation and increase urine production. These properties make it effective in treating hematuria, a condition characterized by the presence of blood in urine, by promoting the flushing out of toxins and reducing irritation in the urinary tract. Moreover, buchu tea has been historically used for treating kidney and bladder ailments, and current research supports its potential in inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections, a common cause of hematuria. Buchu tea's active components, such as diosmin and hesperidin, also possess antioxidant effects that can protect the urinary tract from damage, further reducing the risk of hematuria. Lastly, buchu tea is a natural remedy with few side effects, making it a suitable option for managing hematuria, particularly when used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that tighten the urinary tract, reducing the risk of blood in urine.
Roots: Have anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate irritation in the urinary tract.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that improve kidney function, preventing hematuria.
Seeds: Act as a diuretic that helps flush out bacteria and toxins, reducing the likelihood of blood in urine.
tea_constituents: Diosphenol: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help with hematuria.
Rutin: It strengthens capillary walls, which can help prevent the breaking of blood vessels in the urinary tract and thus alleviate hematuria.
Hesperidin: It improves circulation and has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract and improve hematuria.
Diosmin: It improves venous tone and reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate hematuria by improving circulation in the urinary tract.
Quercetin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, thereby improving hematuria.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot on high heat for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried buchu leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the buchu leaves.
Consume the tea while warm, 3 times a day for hematuria relief.

teas.tea_desc: Parsley tea aids hematuria because it contains apiol and myristicin, compounds with diuretic properties that increase urine production, potentially flushing out blood in the urine. Moreover, parsley's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agents, such as eugenol and limonene, can reduce inflammation and fight infections in the urinary tract, possibly addressing causes of hematuria. Parsley tea also boasts vitamin C and A, which boost the immune system and promote tissue repair, aiding the healing process in the urinary tract. Furthermore, it has APIs, flavonoids with anticoagulant properties that can prevent blood clots and reduce the likelihood of blood in urine. Lastly, parsley tea's high chlorophyll content helps neutralize toxins and excess acidity in the urine, possibly addressing another potential cause of hematuria.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain active compounds that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating hematuria.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that promote kidney health and help control hematuria.
Stems: Have diuretic properties that assist in flushing out toxins and blood from the urinary system.
Flowers: Contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, providing relief from hematuria symptoms.
tea_constituents: Apiol: A compound that acts as a diuretic, which helps flush out the urinary system and reduce the risk of hematuria.
Myristicin: An antioxidant that reduces inflammation and supports kidney health, thereby reducing the risk of hematuria.
Flavonoids: Antioxidant compounds that improve kidney function and reduce the risk of bleeding in the urinary tract.
Limonene: A compound that supports kidney health and helps reduce inflammation, thereby preventing hematuria.
Eugenol: A compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can alleviate symptoms of hematuria.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can prevent and alleviate hematuria.
tea_recipe: Crush 2 tablespoons of dried parsley leaves in a mortar and pestle, releasing their oils.
Place the crushed parsley in a teapot and add 2 cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 10 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent parsley.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, between meals, to help treat hematuria.

teas.tea_desc: Marshmallow tea alleviates hematuria due to its demulcent properties, which come from the mucilage in the plant's roots. This mucilage forms a protective layer on the mucous membranes of the urinary tract, reducing irritation and inflammation. As a result, this soothing effect helps to stop blood in the urine by promoting healing in the affected areas. Furthermore, marshmallow tea boasts diuretic qualities, encouraging the body to flush out toxins and excess fluids, thereby supporting overall urinary health. Finally, its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties help to ward off infections and maintain a healthy urinary tract, further contributing to the elimination of hematuria.
tea_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that act as astringents, helping to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the urinary tract.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate hematuria symptoms.
Flowers: Possess diuretic properties that promote urination, helping to flush out infected or inflamed tissues in the urinary tract.
Seeds: Contain mucilaginous compounds that can soothe irritated tissues and help control bleeding in the urinary tract.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: This constituent forms a protective film over mucous membranes, helping to soothe and reduce irritation in the urinary tract.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in the relief of hematuria caused by inflammation or irritation in the urinary tract.
Ascorbic acid: A natural antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation, promoting healing of damaged tissue in the urinary tract.
Pectin: This soluble fiber has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of hematuria caused by inflammation.
Tannins: These compounds can help to reduce inflammation and have an astringent effect, constricting damaged tissue and reducing the flow of blood in the urinary tract.
tea_recipe: Steep 1 teaspoon of marshmallow root in 1 cup of boiling water for 10 minutes to extract its anti-inflammatory properties.
Strain the tea to remove the marshmallow root, retaining only the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of organic honey to the tea for added soothing effects and improved taste.
Mix 1/4 teaspoon of culinary-grade matcha powder into the tea for its antioxidant benefits.
Consume this marshmallow tea three times a day, between meals, to potentially help manage hematuria.

teas.tea_desc: Dandelion tea helps with hematuria due to its diuretic properties, which increase urine production and flow, flushing out the urinary system and reducing the presence of blood in urine. Its high vitamin C content aids in the restoration of damaged tissues in the urinary tract, while its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate inflammation and irritation in the bladder and kidneys, promoting overall urinary health and preventing recurring hematuria. Additionally, dandelion tea contains antioxidants that fight free radicals, supporting the body's ability to heal and maintain healthy kidney and bladder function, ultimately addressing the underlying causes of hematuria.
tea_parts: Roots: Contain numerous beneficial compounds, including taraxasterol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hematuria.
Leaves: Rich in vitamins and minerals that promote kidney health, helping to reduce hematuria by strengthening the urinary system.
Flowers: High in antioxidants that support kidney and bladder function, reducing the risk of hematuria by protecting against damage.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids, which help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating hematuria symptoms.
tea_constituents: Potassium: Helps regulate fluid balance and may reduce the occurrence of hematuria by promoting proper kidney function.
Vitamin A: Acts as an antioxidant, protecting kidney tissues and promoting cell growth, which can help heal damaged tissues causing hematuria.
Vitamin C: Enhances iron absorption and acts as an antioxidant, supporting overall kidney health and reducing hematuria symptoms.
Tannins: Possess astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and bleeding in the urinary tract responsible for hematuria.
Sesquiterpene lactones: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and possible antispasmodic effects, which can alleviate discomfort and promote healing in cases of hematuria.
tea_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, ensuring they are grown in a pesticide-free environment.
Rinse the dandelion parts thoroughly under cold running water to remove any dirt or insects.
Coarsely chop the dandelion flowers and leaves, then place them in a heatproof pot or teapot.
Pour 4 cups of boiling water over the dandelion pieces, cover, and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent dandelion material, and drink it warm, up to three times a day to help treat hematuria.

teas.tea_desc: Goldenrod tea addresses hematuria, or blood in urine, due to its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. This herbal tea contains flavonoids and saponins, which alleviate inflammation in the urinary tract, reducing blood in urine. Additionally, goldenrod tea stimulates kidney function, increasing urine production and flushing out toxins. The astringent qualities of goldenrod further promote healing by constricting damaged tissues and stopping bleeding. Consequently, goldenrod tea offers a natural remedy for hematuria by soothing inflammation, promoting urination, and aiding in tissue repair.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that help tighten blood vessels, reducing the amount of blood in urine.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate irritation in the urinary tract.
Roots: Rich in tannins that have astringent properties, helping to control bleeding and soothe mucous membranes.
Seeds: Contain oils with anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and irritation.
Bark: Has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections in the urinary tract.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants reduce inflammation and strengthen blood vessels, which can help stop the release of blood in urine caused by hematuria.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, alleviating hematuria symptoms.
Phenolic acids: With their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, phenolic acids can help relieve pain and inflammation associated with hematuria, while promoting healing.
Essential oils: The antibacterial properties of essential oils in goldenrod tea can help prevent and treat urinary tract infections, a common cause of hematuria.
Tannins: Astringent tannins in goldenrod tea help constrict blood vessels and promote blood clotting, reducing the occurrence of hematuria.
Polysaccharides: These compounds have immunostimulant and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding the body's response to urinary tract infections and reducing hematuria symptoms.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot, then add 2 tablespoons of dried goldenrod flowers.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent flowers.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste for improved flavor.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for hematuria relief.

teas.tea_desc: Saw palmetto tea addresses hematuria, or blood in urine, by targeting its common causes. This tea's potent properties stem from the extract of the saw palmetto fruit, which contains compounds such as fatty acids and plant sterols. These compounds work synergistically to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, a common cause of hematuria. Additionally, saw palmetto tea helps strengthen the urinary tract walls, minimizing the chances of irritation and subsequent bleeding. Furthermore, this tea boasts diuretic properties, promoting increased urination and flushing out harmful toxins. Consequently, saw palmetto tea alleviates hematuria by addressing both its symptoms and underlying causes, offering a holistic approach to managing this condition.
tea_parts: Berries: Contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve urinary tract health, reducing symptoms of hematuria.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants that support kidney function, promoting healthy urination and addressing the cause of hematuria.
Rhizomes: Known to contain anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate irritation in the urinary tract and bladder, reducing blood in urine.
tea_constituents: Ursolic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce prostaglandin synthesis and thus alleviate hematuria caused by inflammation.
Oleanolic acid: Possesses anti-proliferative effects on prostate cells, which can help reduce pressure on the urinary tract and alleviate hematuria caused by prostate enlargement.
Beta-sitosterol: Known for its diuretic effects, which can increase urine flow and help flush out potential irritants, relieving hematuria caused by urinary tract infections or inflammation.

Epicatechin: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, providing relief for hematuria related to conditions like interstitial cystitis or bladder infections.
Quercetin: Shows potential for reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thus alleviating hematuria by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.

Kaempferol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help prevent oxidative damage and reduce inflammation, offering relief for hematuria caused by inflammation or oxidative stress.

Myricetin: Known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress, making it useful in treating hematuria caused by various urinary tract conditions.
tea_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried saw palmetto berries.
Place the berries in a tea infuser and steep in boiling water for 15 minutes.
Remove the infuser and let the tea cool to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey for added sweetness, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of saw palmetto tea daily for hematuria relief.

teas.tea_desc: Juliantia Regosa tea aids hematuria because it contains tannins, a type of polyphenolic compound known to have astringent properties. Astringents can constrict damaged tissue, reducing the release of blood into urine. Additionally, Juliantia Regosa tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help heal the urinary tract lining and alleviate irritation. The tea's soothing effect on the urinary system further decreases the risk of hematuria by reducing the strain on the urinary tract. Therefore, consuming Juliantia Regosa tea offers a natural solution to manage hematuria by promoting tissue repair and reducing blood in urine.
tea_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that act as astringents, helping to reduce bleeding in the urinary tract and alleviate hematuria.
Leaves: Rich in tannins and antioxidants, the leaves help soothe inflammation and promote healing in the urinary system.
Flowers: High in anti-inflammatory properties, flowers aid in reducing irritation and swelling in the urinary tract, easing hematuria symptoms.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, seeds offer antimicrobial and diuretic effects, which help eliminate toxins and bacteria from the urinary system.
tea_constituents: Tannins: Tannins have an astringent effect and help to shrink and constrict blood vessels, which can reduce bleeding and subsequently hematuria.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, thus alleviating hematuria.

Epicatechin: Epicatechin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of abnormal cells, thereby reducing the likelihood of hematuria.
Caffeic Acid: Caffeic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the risk of hematuria.

Quercetin: Quercetin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of abnormal cells, thereby reducing the likelihood of hematuria.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes.
Add 3 grams of Juliantia Regosa leaves to the boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Discard the leaves and let the tea cool.
Drink the entire portion of tea, 2-3 times daily.
